title: Initialize Next.js project foundation
description: Set up the base Next.js App Router project with TypeScript, Tailwind CSS, ESLint, src directory structure, and a clean foundation for public and admin routes.
acceptance_criteria: Next.js App Router project is initialized with TypeScript
The src directory structure is created
The project runs locally without errors
notes: Do not add unnecessary dependencies.

title: Scaffold repository structure and developer tooling
description: Create the initial folder structure for the application, public assets, Supabase workspace, and deterministic developer scripts. Add starter environment documentation and basic setup guidance.
acceptance_criteria: The src/app structure includes public and admin route areas
Shared folders for components, lib, types, and placeholder content exist
The public directory contains starter asset folders
The supabase directory contains a migrations folder
setup, lint, typecheck, test, build, security, and validate_pr scripts exist
notes: This task establishes the agreed project structure, deterministic validation scripts, and basic developer tooling before feature work begins.

title: Configure Supabase integration
description: Connect the app to Supabase and create reusable helpers for server-side and client-side usage.
acceptance_criteria: Shared Supabase helper utilities are created
Server-side and client-side access patterns are defined
notes: Never hardcode secrets.

title: Create initial database schema
description: Create the initial schema for site settings, news posts, events, staff members, resources, contact submissions, and admissions inquiries.
acceptance_criteria: Primary keys and timestamps are included
Basic relationships are defined where needed
The schema aligns with MVP scope only
notes: Do not add gradebook, payments, or attendance tables.

title: Add database security and RLS policies
description: Implement Row Level Security and initial access rules for admin-managed content and protected data tables.
acceptance_criteria: RLS is enabled on protected tables
Policies are defined for admin and superadmin access
Public read access is limited to intended public content only
Protected tables are not writable by guests
notes: Favor least-privilege access.

title: Build shared UI shell
description: Create the global navbar, footer, page container, and shared section layout components for the site using a corporate and premium visual direction.
acceptance_criteria: Design is mobile-first and visually polished
notes: Use consistent spacing, reusable components, and a professional premium school aesthetic.

title: Build homepage MVP
description: Create the homepage with hero section, mission statement, quick links, featured news and events preview, and calls to action.
acceptance_criteria: Featured news and events preview is included
The page is responsive and visually polished
notes: Use placeholder content where needed.

title: Build About Us page
description: Create the About Us page with mission, vision, history, and leadership sections.
notes: Keep presentation professional and school-appropriate.

title: Build Admissions page
description: Create the Admissions page with application overview, tuition and fees section, FAQs, and inquiry or tour CTA.
acceptance_criteria: Inquiry or tour CTA is clearly visible
notes: Do not include payments.

title: Build Academics page
description: Create the Academics page with curriculum overview and school-level breakdown for nursery through senior high.
acceptance_criteria: Department or division sections are clearly organized
notes: Keep structure simple and readable.

title: Build Contact Us page
description: Create the Contact Us page with school contact details, location, and inquiry form UI.
acceptance_criteria: School address or location section exists
notes: Submission wiring happens in a later task.

title: Build News and Events page shell
description: Create the public News and Events page structure with placeholder cards and reusable list layout.
acceptance_criteria: News and events listing layout exists
Cards or list items are reusable
The structure supports future dynamic content
notes: Use static placeholders initially.

title: Build Parent Resources page
description: Create a parent resource hub for menus, handbook links, forms, and other helpful school resources.
acceptance_criteria: Parent resource sections are clearly organized
Placeholder resource cards or links are included
The design is easy to scan on mobile
notes: This is not a full parent portal.

title: Build Staff Directory page shell
description: Create the Staff Directory page structure with placeholder staff cards and space for contact details.
acceptance_criteria: The structure supports future dynamic data
notes: Search and filter can come later if needed.

title: Build Policies and Safeguarding page
description: Create a page for school policies, privacy notice, safeguarding content, and important compliance documents.
acceptance_criteria: Downloadable or linked resources can be displayed
notes: Use placeholder documents or links if needed.

title: Implement contact form submission flow
description: Connect the contact form to server-side validation and persist submissions to the database.
acceptance_criteria: The record is stored in the database
An error state is handled gracefully
notes: Avoid client-only sensitive logic.

title: Implement admissions inquiry and tour request flow
description: Create the admissions inquiry workflow and optional tour request submission with secure server-side handling.
acceptance_criteria: Tour request fields are handled if included
The user receives success or error feedback
notes: Keep the form simple for MVP.

title: Set up admin authentication and protected routes
description: Implement admin and superadmin login flow with protected dashboard routes.
acceptance_criteria: Admin-only content is not visible to guests
Role checks support admin and superadmin
notes: MVP roles are guest, admin, and superadmin.

title: Build admin dashboard shell
description: Create the core admin dashboard layout, navigation, and section placeholders for content management.
acceptance_criteria: Navigation for core content areas exists
notes: Keep the admin UI simple and maintainable.

title: Build news management admin feature
description: Allow admins to create, edit, publish, and remove news posts from the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Admins can edit existing news posts
Admins can publish or unpublish posts
The UI handles validation and errors
notes: Keep the content model structured.

title: Build event management admin feature
description: Allow admins to create, edit, and manage school events from the dashboard.
acceptance_criteria: Event data persists to the database
Validation is applied to required fields
The UI handles success and error states
notes: Support title, date, time, location, and description.

title: Build staff management admin feature
description: Allow admins to create and update staff directory entries from the dashboard.
notes: Include role or title and optional contact info.

title: Render dynamic news and events on public pages
description: Replace placeholder content with real news and event data on the homepage and news and events page.
acceptance_criteria: Homepage reads featured news and events from the database
News and Events page reads dynamic content from the database
Content renders correctly on server-side pages
notes: Prefer server-side data fetching.

title: Render dynamic staff directory
description: Replace placeholder staff cards with real staff data from the database.
acceptance_criteria: Staff directory reads from the database
Public rendering is responsive and readable
notes: Keep fields aligned with admin-managed content.

title: Build homepage and site settings management
description: Allow admins to manage selected homepage content and global site settings.
acceptance_criteria: Admins can update selected homepage content
Admins can update global site settings such as contact details
Public pages reflect updated content correctly
notes: Keep editable content scoped and structured.

title: Build resources management and rendering
description: Allow admins to manage downloadable resources and render them on the Parent Resources and Policies pages.
acceptance_criteria: Admins can create and update resource entries
Resources render on the correct public pages
Links or files are stored correctly
notes: Storage integration can be simple in MVP.

title: Add SEO foundations
description: Add page titles, descriptions, social metadata, and other foundational SEO improvements across the public site.
acceptance_criteria: Open Graph or equivalent metadata is added where appropriate
notes: Keep SEO practical and lightweight.

title: Perform accessibility and responsive polish pass
description: Review and improve mobile responsiveness, keyboard usability, semantic structure, and form accessibility.
acceptance_criteria: Major pages are usable on mobile screens
Forms have labels and accessible states
notes: Treat this as a release-quality sweep.

title: Add loading, empty, and error states
description: Improve user experience by handling loading, no-data, and error scenarios across public and admin flows.
acceptance_criteria: Dynamic pages handle missing data gracefully
Admin flows display meaningful success and error feedback
User-facing messages are clear and professional
notes: Do not leave broken blank states.

title: Prepare deployment and production readiness
description: Finalize environment configuration, confirm build stability, and prepare the project for deployment.
notes: This is the final MVP readiness check.
